other_observers: Sean Walters, Cheryl Teuton, Dan Brooke -- all agreed with ID
description: Larger than there neighboring HERG, bigger chested, thicker billed, shorter winged. Uniform in "lightness" as seen from a lateral view while perched. The primaries were no darker than the tertials nor the body. Rather pale, pale enough so to make me think ICGU for a moment. Both birds had gray backs that were similar in darkness to the brown body. The patterning seemed smudgy. When the bird flapped we could see uniformly colored wings (no dark on primaries, secondaries) and a white and dark patterned (barred or perhaps spotted dark on white background) rump and solid pale smoky tail -- the tail, same darkness as remainder of body. Bill all black.
